questions.question: What is the primary goal of exploring two or more variables?
questions.explanation: Exploring two or more variables helps to visualize relationships between variables, identify correlations, and understand the distribution of variables.

questions.question: Which method is commonly used to visualize the relationship between two continuous variables?
questions.explanation: Scatterplots are used to visualize the relationship between two continuous variables, showing the distribution and correlation of the data.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a heat map in exploring two or more variables?
options: To visualize the distribution of one variable
To show the relationship between two categorical variables
To identify patterns and correlations between variables
questions.explanation: Heat maps are used to show the relationship between two categorical variables, highlighting patterns and correlations between the variables.

questions.question: Which statistical method can be used to analyze the relationship between two or more categorical variables?
questions.explanation: Crosstabulation is used to analyze the relationship between two or more categorical variables, showing the distribution and correlation of the data.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of exploring two or more variables?
questions.explanation: The main advantage of exploring two or more variables is to identify relationships and patterns, which can inform future data analysis and decision-making.
